title: Speak Up for Stuttering Awareness Day
story: Every October 22, people around the world celebrate International Stuttering Awareness Day to support friends who stutter when they speak. Schools host special assemblies where kids learn that stuttering is totally normal and nothing to worry about. Many famous actors, singers, and athletes stutter too! People wear blue ribbons, share encouraging messages online, and practice being patient listeners. It's a day to remind everyone that what you say matters way more than how you say it.
activity_question: What do people wear to show support on International Stuttering Awareness Day?
follow_up_question: How can you be a better listener when someone takes their time to speak?

title: CAPS LOCK DAY: WHEN SHOUTING IS ALLOWED!
story: Every October 22, computer users around the world celebrate the most dramatic key on their keyboard during Caps Lock Day! People intentionally type EVERYTHING IN CAPITAL LETTERS to honor this often-misunderstood button. Social media fills with ALL-CAPS posts, emails get EXTRA LOUD, and friends playfully "yell" at each other through their screens. Some people share their funniest autocorrect fails or times they accidentally left Caps Lock on. Others post motivational quotes that look like they're being shouted from rooftops. It's the one day when typing in all capitals isn't considered rude - it's actually encouraged!
activity_question: What do people do on Caps Lock Day to celebrate?
activity_choices: Delete the Caps Lock key from keyboards
follow_up_question: What's the funniest thing that could happen if you forgot Caps Lock was on?

title: Nacho Average Holiday!
story: Chips covered in melted cheese never tasted so official! October 22 is International Day of the Nacho, when people around the world celebrate this crunchy, cheesy snack that started in a small Mexican border town. Restaurants offer special nacho deals, families make giant platters at home, and social media fills with photos of creative nacho creations. Some people stick to classic cheese and jalapeños, while others pile on everything from pulled pork to pineapple. Food trucks join the fun with nacho-themed specials, and many schools even serve nachos in their cafeterias on this delicious day.
activity_question: What do restaurants typically do to celebrate International Day of the Nacho?
bonus_fact: The first nachos were invented in 1943 by a chef named Ignacio "Nacho" Anaya.

title: Wombat Day: Celebrating Cube-Pooping Cuties
story: Every October 22, animal lovers around the world celebrate Wombat Day to honor Australia's adorable, chunky marsupials! People share wombat photos and videos on social media, showing off these furry bulldozers as they waddle around and dig burrows. Zoos host special wombat talks where visitors learn amazing facts, like how wombats poop in perfect cubes and can run up to 25 miles per hour despite their chubby appearance. Many fans wear wombat-themed shirts or donate to Australian wildlife conservation groups. It's the perfect day to appreciate these surprisingly speedy, square-pooping superstars!
activity_question: What unusual shape is wombat poop?
bonus_fact: Wombats have backwards-facing pouches so dirt doesn't get in while digging!
